# T-055: MDI patient creation + minimal DynamoDB linkage

Generated from `docs/external/MD Integrations API.postman_collection.json`. The raw Postman collection remains authoritative; use this pack as a retrieval guide, not as a replacement for source verification.

Use partner patient endpoints to create or retrieve the MDI patient ID, then persist only the Cognito-to-MDI linkage/status record in DynamoDB.

## Endpoint Matrix

| Operation | Method | Path | Surface | Ticket purpose | Required local persistence | Forbidden local persistence |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [partner-post-partner-patients-create-patient](../operations/partner-post-partner-patients-create-patient.md) | POST | /partner/patients | partner | Use partner patient endpoints to create or retrieve the MDI patient ID, then persist only the Cognito-to-MDI linkage/status record in DynamoDB. | Required local persistence: Cognito subject, MDI patient ID, onboarding/linkage status, timestamps, and non-PHI idempotency state if needed. | Forbidden local persistence: patient clinical profile payloads, source patient examples, SSN, questionnaire answers, clinical content, and PHI-heavy MDI response bodies. |
| [partner-get-v1-partner-patients-patient-id-get-patient](../operations/partner-get-v1-partner-patients-patient-id-get-patient.md) | GET | /v1/partner/patients/:patient_id | partner | Use partner patient endpoints to create or retrieve the MDI patient ID, then persist only the Cognito-to-MDI linkage/status record in DynamoDB. | Required local persistence: Cognito subject, MDI patient ID, onboarding/linkage status, timestamps, and non-PHI idempotency state if needed. | Forbidden local persistence: patient clinical profile payloads, source patient examples, SSN, questionnaire answers, clinical content, and PHI-heavy MDI response bodies. |
| [partner-post-v1-partner-patients-search-search-patients](../operations/partner-post-v1-partner-patients-search-search-patients.md) | POST | /v1/partner/patients/search | partner | Use partner patient endpoints to create or retrieve the MDI patient ID, then persist only the Cognito-to-MDI linkage/status record in DynamoDB. | Required local persistence: Cognito subject, MDI patient ID, onboarding/linkage status, timestamps, and non-PHI idempotency state if needed. | Forbidden local persistence: patient clinical profile payloads, source patient examples, SSN, questionnaire answers, clinical content, and PHI-heavy MDI response bodies. |
| [partner-patch-partner-patients-patient-id-update-patient](../operations/partner-patch-partner-patients-patient-id-update-patient.md) | PATCH | /partner/patients/:patient_id | partner | Use partner patient endpoints to create or retrieve the MDI patient ID, then persist only the Cognito-to-MDI linkage/status record in DynamoDB. | Required local persistence: Cognito subject, MDI patient ID, onboarding/linkage status, timestamps, and non-PHI idempotency state if needed. | Forbidden local persistence: patient clinical profile payloads, source patient examples, SSN, questionnaire answers, clinical content, and PHI-heavy MDI response bodies. |

## Thin-PHI Notes

- Required local persistence: Cognito subject, MDI patient ID, onboarding/linkage status, timestamps, and non-PHI idempotency state if needed.
- Forbidden local persistence: patient clinical profile payloads, source patient examples, SSN, questionnaire answers, clinical content, and PHI-heavy MDI response bodies.
- Do not put PHI, clinical content, questionnaire answers, or MDI payload fragments in Stripe metadata or logs.
- Prefer `partner` and `webhook` routes. Internal/admin/test/unknown routes are default-deny unless a future ticket explicitly justifies them.

## Source Provenance

- `partner-post-partner-patients-create-patient`: Partners / Patients / Create patient
- `partner-get-v1-partner-patients-patient-id-get-patient`: Partners / Patients / Get patient
- `partner-post-v1-partner-patients-search-search-patients`: Partners / Patients / Search patients
- `partner-patch-partner-patients-patient-id-update-patient`: Partners / Patients / Update patient
